source: Buy-side diligence question list (demo). Pillars and questions grounded in the topics raised on Apple's Q2 FY26 earnings call (Apr 30, 2026).
thesis: AAPL enters the print mid-supercycle: the iPhone 17 family is driving 20%+ revenue growth — the strongest cycle in company history — while Mac demand (MacBook Neo, plus mini/Studio as local-AI machines) runs ahead of supply. Growth is gated by supply, not demand: the binding constraint is advanced-node SOC availability (iPhone in the March quarter, shifting to Mac into June). The central risk is the memory-cost overhang, modest now but building into a growing gross-margin headwind beyond June. Services keeps compounding at high margin with advertising (App Store, now Maps) as a fresh lever; capital allocation is shifting as Apple drops the net-cash-neutral target and lifts buyback authorization by $100B; AI strategy pairs a Google partnership with in-house foundational models on accelerating R&D; and management hands the CEO role to John Ternus in September.

questions.pillar: Supply-gated hardware supercycle: iPhone and Mac demand is outrunning supply, with broad geographic strength
questions.question: How strong and durable is the hardware cycle (iPhone 17 family, MacBook Neo, Mac mini / Studio), how binding are the supply constraints, and how broad is demand across Greater China and India?
complete_when: Addresses iPhone growth, its drivers, and durability
Addresses the Mac / MacBook Neo cycle and new-to-Mac customers
Addresses where supply constraints bind (advanced-node SOCs; iPhone in the March quarter, Mac into June) and the path to balance
Addresses geographic breadth (Greater China and India)

expected.note_models: iPhone +~22% (strongest cycle ever) led by the 17 family; MacBook Neo off the charts with a record for customers new to Mac; constraints are advanced-node SOC availability (iPhone in the March quarter, Mac into June); Greater China first half +33% (quarterly revenue record), India a long runway at still-modest share
expected.our_view: Demand is genuinely outrunning supply across iPhone and Mac, with China re-accelerating and India the structural multi-year story
expected.watch: Whether iPhone is called constrained into June, the upgrader-versus-switcher mix, and the Mac mini / Studio rebalancing timeline

questions.pillar: Margin and capital: the memory-cost overhang on gross margin against a shift in the capital-return framework
questions.question: Where does gross margin trend given the building memory-cost headwind (net of tariffs, mix, FX), and what does dropping the net-cash-neutral target mean for capital return?
complete_when: States company gross margin and the sequential bridge (mix, tariffs, memory, leverage, FX)
Characterizes the memory-cost trajectory beyond June and the offsets / range of options
Explains the move off net-cash-neutral and the buyback / investment posture

expected.note_models: Company GM ~49%; sequential bridge helped by favorable mix and lower tariffs, hurt by seasonal deleverage and memory, FX roughly neutral; memory a building headwind beyond June; net-cash-neutral retired as a formal target (cash and debt evaluated independently); +$100B buyback authorization; over $1T returned to date
expected.our_view: Mix and tariff relief are masking a memory bill that grows through FY26; the capital-allocation change is added flexibility, not a buyback retreat
expected.bear: An escalating memory cost that pricing can't fully pass through compresses product gross margin even as buybacks continue

questions.pillar: The forward call: high-margin Services and advertising, an in-house-plus-Google AI strategy, and the CEO handoff to John Ternus
questions.question: How durable are Services and the emerging advertising lever, what is Apple's AI / foundational-model strategy and the investment behind it, and how clean is the CEO transition to John Ternus?
complete_when: Addresses Services growth / margin and advertising (App Store search ads, upcoming Maps ads)
Addresses the AI strategy (in-house versus Google) and the R&D / investment behind it
Addresses the CEO transition timing and continuity of strategy and capital discipline

expected.note_models: Services compounding at high margin (services GM +~20bps sequentially on mix); advertising up YoY with new App Store inventory and Apple Maps ads launching in US / Canada this summer; two-pronged AI (Google collaboration plus in-house foundational models) on accelerating R&D; John Ternus becomes CEO in September with messaging stressing continuity
expected.our_view: Services and ads are the high-margin compounder while AI is a deliberate, privacy-led investment; the succession is orderly and telegraphed
expected.watch: Whether ad inventory moves the needle, whether Google dependence deepens or AI yields a new form factor, and any posture change under the new CEO
